evals.prompt: we just figured out that the export pipeline crashes when the input DataFrame is empty — missing empty-check before column iteration. I want to log this as an Asana ticket before we dig in and fix it
evals.expected_output: Skill infers Plan Only. Loads the board registry cache from ~/.claude/asana-workflow/<project-key>.json — if missing, runs Full Discovery (queries workspace projects, classifies sprint vs backlog boards, writes cache). Discovers custom fields dynamically from the sprint project. Presents a full draft with title ≤72 chars, description capturing the root cause, priority set to highest available, sizing/estimate set to lowest, assignee unset (plan-only), product status = Assigned. Shows field options in brackets. Shows suggested backlog boards ranked by relevance with pre-selected matches, plus the auto-detected active sprint. User must confirm board selection and fields before any API calls. After confirmation, creates task and adds to the active sprint + each confirmed backlog board, fetches the task ID, reports success with Asana URL listing all boards, then asks if user wants to invoke start-task.

evals.prompt: ok we just fixed the auth middleware bug — session tokens weren't being invalidated on logout because the Redis TTL wasn't being reset. I already updated the middleware and added a test. let's get this logged in asana and ship it
evals.expected_output: Skill infers Fix Done. Loads board registry cache (or runs Full Discovery if missing). Discovers fields. In the draft, assignee = current user, sizing/estimate reflect a small-to-medium fix (not the absolute lowest since there was real work done). Shows suggested backlog boards ranked by relevance with pre-selected matches, plus auto-detected active sprint. After confirmation, creates task and adds to the active sprint + each confirmed backlog board, fetches task ID, then asks [ship/later] BEFORE touching git. If ship: creates a git worktree sibling to the repo on a new branch named <task-id>/fix-auth-session-invalidation (or similar slug), copies the changed files into the worktree, commits them, then invokes ship-it with the Asana task context threaded through.

evals.prompt: can you create an asana task for the CSV null pointer issue we were looking at?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Because it's ambiguous whether work is done, the skill asks a single clarifying question to determine the variant before proceeding. Once variant is established, follows the appropriate flow. Does not ask for information already in the conversation (e.g., the issue description). Shows board confirmation + field draft before creating anything.
